item.title: @elonmusk: Tesla has just released a new app update. Clips downloaded to your phone now finally include details...
item.description: Tesla has just released a new app update. Clips downloaded to your phone now finally include details such as speed, steering wheel angle, and Self-Driving state. No more screen recording FSD clips! Here’s what it looks like:

item.title: @elonmusk: Model S with over 800,000 kilometers on the odometer still going strong!
item.description: Model S with over 800,000 kilometers on the odometer still going strong!

item.title: @elonmusk: Grok just hit a new all-time high on web traffic. 🔥 More than 326 million visits last month. More pe...
item.description: Grok just hit a new all-time high on web traffic. 🔥 More than 326 million visits last month. More people are switching to Grok.

item.title: @elonmusk: NEWS: Senior Government Members Lie About Starlink in South Africa Parliamentary chair Khusela Diko ...
item.description: NEWS: Senior Government Members Lie About Starlink in South Africa Parliamentary chair Khusela Diko claims Starlink’s FREE high-speed internet for 5,000 rural schools “brings nothing to the table” and “won’t move the needle an inch.” The Truth: Starlink is putting up R500 million of its own cash for zero-cost kits, lightning-fast internet, and full maintenance, forever. This connects 2.4 million rural kids in deep countryside areas where cables and towers can’t reach. Kids get online classes, free learning videos, homework help, and real skills for the future. Teachers access better resources. Entire villages finally join the digital world, boosting education, jobs, and hope where it’s needed most.@elonmuskis stepping up with real help for free while politicians downplay it. Stop the lies — approve Starlink NOW!

item.title: @elonmusk: This chip is just… beautiful. Tesla AI5
item.description: This chip is just… beautiful. Tesla AI5

item.title: @elonmusk: Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas on Wednesday delivered a televised broadside against progressi...
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas on Wednesday delivered a televised broadside against progressivism, a political philosophy he described as an existential threat to America and the principles that founded it 250 years ago.abcnews.link/vKHBiTS

item.title: @elonmusk: most powerful moving object in all of human history hard to comprehend
item.description: most powerful moving object in all of human history hard to comprehend

item.title: @elonmusk: This is what a black politician in South Africa said… “We will kill white women, we will kill white ...
item.description: This is what a black politician in South Africa said… “We will kill white women, we will kill white children, and we will even kill your pets" Violent language entering politics is a serious warning sign. When public figures normalize targeting civilians, it erodes the foundation of any society. South Africa built its identity on reconciliation and rule of law, and moments like this raise real questions about how far that standard is being tested. Elites love turning@elonmuskinto the villain, especially when he speaks about the reality of crime, division, and failing governance in the country he was born into. It’s easier to attack the messenger than confront uncomfortable facts. There is a real problem when violent rhetoric enters public discourse and gets brushed aside or normalized. Any language that targets civilians, any group of civilians, is a red line. Calling that out isn’t extremism, it’s basic principle. The real question isn’t why Elon Musk speaks up. The real question is why those raising concerns about safety, accountability, and equal protection under the law are dismissed, while dangerous rhetoric gets space to grow? A country built on reconciliation cannot afford selective outrage.xcancel.com/realpatriotM/status/20…

item.title: @elonmusk: 🇿🇦 A senior South African politician just got caught lying about Starlink to protect mobile network ...
item.description: 🇿🇦 A senior South African politician just got caught lying about Starlink to protect mobile network operators. Parliamentary communications chair Khusela Diko claimed Starlink "doesn't move the needle" on school connectivity. Here's what she left out: 16,000 schools still have no internet after 12 years, a missed deadline, and mobile operators billions over budget. Starlink offered to connect 5,000 schools for free and was turned away. A rural mobile tower costs around $61,000 and can serve just a single school. The numbers don't lie. The politician did. Source: MyBroadband

item.title: @elonmusk: Look at the tiny cars in the foreground to get a sense of the vastness of the building
item.description: Look at the tiny cars in the foreground to get a sense of the vastness of the building
